parsed_review.summary: CP00-382 is a Risk-C, 150-unit descriptor-only billing-core pack (RP12.P06.M09.S03-RP12.P07.M05.S04) that closes the RP12.P06 phase and opens the RP12.P07 failure/foundation scaffolding. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is correctly conservative and fully supported: manifest and command-evidence both set production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false with status implemented_pending_review; the Hermes evidence asserts only production_ready_candidate:true, never final production readiness. Plan, manifest, contract projection, registry (BILLING_CORE_CP382_REQUIREMENTS / _PACK_BINDING / _NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION), service (createBillingCoreCp382* in service.js:2306-2425), validators (validators.js:3491-3658), and the three CP00-382 tests are mutually consistent: 150 units, risk C, correct range, 8 sections, and the exact deliverable distribution (implementation 23, security_audit 19, ui 8, claude_review 5, test 16, failure_recovery 64, hermes_evidence 10, fixture 5). All no-write/no-real-data vectors are attested false and the source contains no fs/db/network/exec I/O — only frozen descriptor literals (synthetic_only:true, no_real_data:true). The read-only reviewer boundary is preserved everywhere (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false), with human final approval required for the CP00-383 runtime opening. CP178+ review hardening is complete: forbidden_review_evidence/invalid_review_blockers reject failed auth, zero-byte, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled-tools runs, missing references, and final-approval/source-mutation/enterprise-trust claims; auth preflight passed and absent raw-output/receipt files are the expected pre-review state. Hermes patterns are confined to the deterministic descriptor review packet/receipt (receipt_shape descriptor_only_..._no_write; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false). Unrelated dirty files (Korean docx, workbook/*, ldip invalid-attempt) are correctly classified as preserved and excluded from pack scope. No P0-P3 findings.
